name: @radzor/video-call
description: WebRTC-based peer-to-peer video calling. Manage local/remote streams, signaling, and call lifecycle.

inputs.name: iceServers
inputs.description: ICE server configuration (default: Google STUN servers).

inputs.name: signalingUrl
inputs.description: WebSocket signaling server URL for exchanging offers and answers.

outputs.name: localStream
outputs.description: Local audio/video stream from the user's camera/microphone.

outputs.name: remoteStream
outputs.description: Remote audio/video stream from the peer.

events.name: onLocalStream
events.description: Fired when local media stream is ready.

events.name: onRemoteStream
events.description: Fired when remote stream arrives from the peer.

events.name: onError
events.description: Fired on ICE, media, or signaling error.

actions.name: startCall
actions.description: Start a call by capturing local media and creating an SDP offer.

actions.name: answerCall

params.name: offer
params.description: SDP offer from the remote peer.

actions.description: Answer an incoming call by setting the remote offer and creating an SDP answer.

actions.name: endCall
actions.description: End the current call and release all media tracks.

actions.name: toggleAudio
actions.description: Toggle microphone mute. Returns the new muted state.

actions.name: toggleVideo
actions.description: Toggle camera on/off. Returns the new enabled state.

llm.constraints: Browser-only — uses WebRTC and getUserMedia APIs not available in Node.js. Requires HTTPS in production. For peer discovery and signaling, a WebSocket server is needed.
